overview: From 2026-06-06 to 2026-06-07, elizaos/eliza had 24 new PRs (11 merged), 0 new issues, and 12 active contributors.

topIssues.title: App-credits: purchases fund the per-app pool but inference debits the org balance (stranded credits)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary
App-credits purchases and inference spend use **two different ledgers**, so credits a user buys for a monetized app can't be spent — and a user with cloud/org credits is blocked from a monetized app unless they separately top up the app pool. This blocks third-party browser apps monetizing via `X-App-Id` end-to-end.

This looks like a half-finished migration (see the `// Switched from app_credit_balances ... to the org balance` comment), so flagging rather than unilaterally changing billing.

## Details (origin/develop)
- **Purchase funds the per-app pool.** `processPurchase` credits `app_credit_balances` via `appCreditBalancesRepository.addCredits(...)` — `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/app-credits.ts:~307`.
- **Balance check reads the per-app pool.** `checkBalance` → `appCreditBalancesRepository.findByAppAndUser(...)` — `app-credits.ts:~193`.
- **Spend debits the ORG balance.** The spend path was switched to `creditsService.reserveAndDeductCredits({ organizationId })` — `app-credits.ts:~408` (the "Switched from app_credit_balances ... to the org balance" comment). The `X-App-Id` completions path also reserves against the org: `reserveCredits({ organizationId })` — `packages/cloud-api/v1/chat/completions/route.ts:~934`.

## Impact
1. A user buys app-credits → funds land in `app_credit_balances` → inference debits the **org** balance → "Insufficient credits" against an empty org balance. **Money paid, credits never spendable.**
2. `checkBalance` gates on the app pool, so a user who *does* have org/cloud credits is rejected from a monetized app unless they top up the app pool too.
3. The `/api/v1/apps/[id]/chat` 402 says "Insufficient app credits" but deducts from the org balance (`route.ts:~295-333`), so the error misdirects users.

## Suggested fix (pick one ledger)
- **A — org-only (matches the "Switched" comment's intent):** make `processPurchase` credit the **org** balance (and have `/api/v1/app-credits/balance` + `checkBalance` report/read org). App-credits checkout becomes "top up cloud credits for this app"; creator still earns markup via `recordCreatorEarnings()`. Simplest if the per-app pool is being retired.
- **B — app-pool-only:** make the `X-App-Id` spend path read+debit `app_credit_balances` instead of the org balance.

Happy to open the PR once you confirm the direction (A or B) — A looks consistent with the existing comment + `recordCreatorEarnings`. Also a one-line follow-up: word the `/apps/[id]/chat` 402 to match whichever ledger is checked.

Found while building a third-party app (SupaKan) on the `X-App-Id` app-credits flow.

topIssues.title: proposal: GET /api/suggestions — server-tailored prompt suggestions for the continuous-chat overlay
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Context
The continuous-chat overlay shows an always-5 prompt-suggestion strip (#8201), now lightly tailored client-side (time-of-day + history; #8224). The next step toward genuinely **context/history/memory-aware** suggestions is a small server endpoint — filing as an issue first (non-trivial, touches `@elizaos/agent`) to coordinate before any PR.

## Proposed design (mirrors the existing `quickContext` pattern)
- **`GET /api/suggestions?scope=<PageScope>&roomId=<uuid>`** in `@elizaos/agent` (handler alongside `handleMemoryRoutes`; `quickContext`/`/api/context/quick` is the blueprint — `packages/agent/src/api/memory-routes.ts`). Returns a typed `{ suggestions: PromptSuggestion[5], tier, generatedAt }`.
- **Client**: `client.getPromptSuggestions(scope, opts)` on `@elizaos/ui` (mirror `client.quickContext`).
- **Wiring**: `usePromptSuggestions` calls it on view-change / convo-switch / message-settled (debounced), with the current **static set as the offline fallback** (no signature change — already structured for this).
- **Tailoring inputs** (all eliza-native, no odysseus backend): active view/route (`app.tab`→`PageScope`), recent thread (`getMemories`), optional memory (`searchMemories`), time of day.
- **Phasing**: Phase-1 = scope + recent-thread heuristic, **zero model calls** (cheap, cacheable); Phase-2 = optional single `TEXT_SMALL` rank. Always returns 5; degrade-not-empty.

## Why an issue, not a PR
This adds a public backend endpoint + client surface — wanted @shawmakesmagic's steer on (a) endpoint shape vs. a provider, and (b) whether this overlaps anything you're already doing, before I build it. Happy to implement once there's a direction.

Refs: #8201 (strip), #8224 (client-side tailoring).

topPRs.title: refactor(lifeops): split plugin-lifeops into focused plugins + plugin-personal-assistant
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Decomposes the ~7.5K-LOC `plugin-lifeops` monolith into a slim orchestrator (`plugin-personal-assistant`) plus 6 new focused plugins, with the `/lifeops` dashboard view removed in favor of per-domain plugin views. Existing domain plugins (todos, health, calendar, documents, phone) gain the owner-facing actions and views they should have owned all along. Stub future homes for runtime primitives (owner-state, scheduled-task, registries, dispatch) are scaffolded in `@elizaos/core` and `@elizaos/app-core` to mark where the still-in-PA implementations will eventually migrate.

No backwards-compat shims — `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` is gone, not aliased.

## What changed

### New plugins (Phase 1)
- **plugin-relationships** — entity/relationship graph (`ENTITY` action + Drizzle `app_relationships` schema). Absorbs lifeops `entities/`, `relationships/`, voice-observer bridge.
- **plugin-inbox** — cross-channel inbox (`INBOX` action + triage + Drizzle `app_inbox` + `/inbox` view). Distinct from plugin-messages (which stays Android-SMS-only).
- **plugin-goals** — life goals + self-care (`OWNER_GOALS`/`ROUTINES`/`REMINDERS`/`ALARMS` + `CheckinService` + Drizzle `app_goals` + `/goals` view).
- **plugin-blocker** — focus/distraction control (`BLOCK` action + website + app blocker services + `/focus` view).
- **plugin-finances** — owner finances (`OWNER_FINANCES` + Drizzle `app_finances` + `/finances` view).
- **plugin-remote-desktop** — `REMOTE_DESKTOP` action.

### Extended plugins (Phase 2)
- **plugin-todos** — `+ /todos` view (Today/Upcoming/Someday lanes); doc-block folds `OWNER_TODOS`.
- **plugin-health** — `+ OWNER_HEALTH`, `OWNER_SCREENTIME` actions; `+ /health` view (Sleep/Circadian/Screen-time/Activity/Connectors).
- **plugin-calendar** — `+ CALENDAR` (8 ops), `CONFLICT_DETECT` actions; `+ /calendar` view.
- **plugin-documents** — `+ OWNER_DOCUMENTS` action; `+ /documents` view.
- **plugin-phone** — `+ VOICE_CALL` (Twilio) action.

### Rename + view kill (Phase 3)
- `plugin-lifeops` → `plugin-personal-assistant` (727+50 file renames, 299 cross-workspace updates, zero remaining `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` references).
- Internal exports renamed (`appLifeOpsPlugin` → `personalAssistantPlugin` etc.).
- `packages/shared/src/contracts/lifeops.ts` → `personal-assistant.ts`.
- App registry entry `apps/lifeops.json` → `apps/personal-assistant.json`.
- `/lifeops` view deleted (LifeOpsPageView, LifeOpsTuiView, XR variant, `lifeops.overview` widget). PA no longer ships a view — domain plugins do.
- `.github/workflows/lifeops-bench*.yml` filenames kept (benchmark-suite identifier, not the plugin name) — required-check refs would break.

### Core foundations (Phase 3, structural only)
Scaffold-only future homes; code still lives in `plugin-personal-assistant` until the next migration pass:
- `packages/core/src/owner-state/` — OwnerFactsStore, HandoffStore, GlobalPauseStore, FirstRunService, PendingPromptsStore interfaces.
- `packages/core/src/registries/` — Anchor, Gate, EventKind, EscalationLadder, Family, Blocker registry interfaces.
- `packages/core/src/scheduled-task/` — ScheduledTask types extending core's existing task-scheduler with gates/escalation/completionCheck/anchors.
- `packages/app-core/src/dispatch/` — ConnectorRegistry, ChannelRegistry, ApprovalQueue, send-policy interfaces.
Each module has a README with `TODO(migrate:)` pointing back to the still-canonical source.

### What stayed in `plugin-personal-assistant`
The cross-domain orchestration role:
- `BRIEF`, `PRIORITIZE`, `PERSONAL_ASSISTANT`, `SCHEDULED_TASKS`, `RESOLVE_REQUEST` actions.
- Default-pack composition (daily-rhythm, morning-brief, followup-starter, habit-starters, executive-assistant).
- The entity/relationship store, scheduled-task runner, owner-state, registries, connector/channel registries — all still here, will migrate into core/app-core in follow-up passes once the new homes exist.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run lint` — exit 0
- [x] `bun run typecheck` — exit 0 workspace-wide
- [x] `bun run build` — exit 0 workspace-wide
- [ ] Manual smoke: load PA + the new domain plugins in dev-ui, confirm each registered view renders
- [ ] Manual smoke: `BRIEF` action still composes calendar + inbox + todos + finances correctly
- [ ] DB migration plan: new plugins introduce `app_relationships`, `app_inbox`, `app_goals`, `app_blocker`, `app_finances` schemas — verify their tables co-exist with PA's existing `app_lifeops` schema during the cut-over window

## Follow-up

This PR establishes the structural decomposition. The remaining migration moves the implementations out of `plugin-personal-assistant` into the new homes:
1. Move EntityStore + RelationshipStore + merge engine → plugin-relationships.
2. Move INBOX action body + TriageRepository → plugin-inbox.
3. Move OWNER_GOALS/ROUTINES/REMINDERS/ALARMS bodies + CheckinService + default-pack fragments → plugin-goals.
4. Move BLOCK + website-blocker/ + app-blocker/ → plugin-blocker.
5. Move OWNER_FINANCES body → plugin-finances.
6. Move CALENDAR/CONFLICT_DETECT bodies + calendar-gate + travel-time → plugin-calendar.
7. Move OWNER_DOCUMENTS body → plugin-documents.
8. Move OWNER_HEALTH/SCREENTIME bodies + ActivityTracker + PresenceSignalBridge → plugin-health.
9. Move ScheduledTaskRunner + Anchor/Gate/EventKind/EscalationLadder registries + OwnerState services → @elizaos/core.
10. Move ConnectorRegistry + ChannelRegistry + ApprovalQueue + send-policy → @elizaos/app-core.
11. After (1)-(10), PA becomes ~10% of its current size — pure BRIEF/PRIORITIZE/PA orchestration + default-pack composition.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): wire the child→parent USE_SKILL parent-agent broker dispatcher
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## What

Closes the production gap that left the capped self-spend autonomy mechanism (merged in #8250) unreachable by a live agent. A `/economics` sub-agent can now autonomously drive the monetized-app loop through the parent-agent Cloud command broker.

## Why

`runParentAgentBroker` / `runCloudCommand` (the only emitter of `spend_auto_authorized`) had **no production caller**: nothing parsed a child's `USE_SKILL parent-agent {…}` request, `buildSkillsManifest({ virtualSkills })` was never called, and the autonomy was only proven via Vitest. See `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/docs/economics-goal-runbook.md` (bundled/updated here).

## How (the three gaps, closed)

1. **Dispatch** — new `parent-agent-dispatch.ts`: a string/escape-aware extractor for `USE_SKILL parent-agent {json}` (handles streaming/partial JSON, braces inside strings, nested params, markdown backticks, malformed markers) + a dispatch helper that runs the directive through `runParentAgentBroker` and streams `result.text` back via `acp.sendToSession`. Wired into `SubAgentRouter.handleEvent`: accumulates the child's `message` chunks, dispatches the first complete directive, caps round-trips via `ACPX_SUB_AGENT_ROUND_TRIP_CAP`. **Marker-guarded** — only acts on text containing `USE_SKILL parent-agent`, which ordinary coding tasks never emit, so the session-event hot path is inert for every other flow.
2. **Advertise** — `spawnAgentForTask` writes a `SKILLS.md` for `capabilityProfile === "economics"` tasks via `buildSkillsManifest(..., { recommendedSlugs: ["build-monetized-app","eliza-cloud"], virtualSkills: [PARENT_AGENT_BROKER_MANIFEST_ENTRY] })` so the child learns the slug + arg contract. Best-effort (never fatal).
3. **Estimate** — the broker's unknown-cost stall now returns an *actionable* instruction (fetch a quote with `domains.check`, retry with `params.spendEstimateUsd`) instead of a human-only yes/no, and the manifest guidance advertises the same pattern — so `domains.buy` self-authorizes within the cap without a human turn.

## Verification

- New `parent-agent-dispatch.test.ts` (11 cases): extractor edge cases + the broker→`sendToSession` bridge end to end via the network-free `list-cloud-commands` mode.
- Existing `parent-agent-broker.test.ts` + `skill-manifest.test.ts` still green (no regression from the preview/guidance change).
- Plugin typecheck clean; biome clean.
- End-to-end (a real `spend_auto_authorized` on `domains.buy`/`containers.create`) still needs a live ACP backend — the runbook documents the exact steps.

## Safety

The hot-path hook is a no-op unless the child emits the literal `USE_SKILL parent-agent` marker. Paid/mutating commands remain gated by the existing capped spend allowance (`ELIZA_AGENT_SPEND_CAP_USD`, default 0 = confirm everything); destructive commands always require a human.

topPRs.title: fix: mobile cloud onboarding
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
## Summary

- fixes Cloud OAuth login from mobile/CLI auth by keeping Steward's `redirect_uri` on the bare allowlisted `/login` callback and storing the post-login return target in session storage
- simplifies the iOS first-run surface for Cloud v1: orange background, orb + transparent `Connect`, no local runtime choice on the compact mobile screen
- waits for native Cloud login completion before provisioning the Cloud agent, then persists the provisioned agent runtime URL/id separately
- adds cloud simulator/device build and sideload helper commands plus focused mobile/cloud onboarding coverage

## Root Cause

The iOS app opens `https://www.elizacloud.ai/auth/cli-login?...` for native Cloud auth. The deployed login page preserved `returnTo` by putting the query string on the Steward OAuth `redirect_uri`, producing `/login?returnTo=...`. Steward's production tenant allowlist accepts `https://www.elizacloud.ai/login` but rejects the query-bearing callback, so the in-app browser landed on a white JSON `redirect_uri is not allowed` error before Google auth.

## Validation

- `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend test src/pages/login/steward-oauth-url.test.ts src/pages/login/login-return-to.test.ts src/pages/login/steward-login-section.test.tsx`
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test src/first-run/use-first-run-controller.test.tsx src/first-run/CompactOnboarding.test.tsx src/api/client-cloud-direct-auth.test.ts src/first-run/first-run.test.ts src/state/persistence-cloud-active-server.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/shared typecheck`
- `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend typecheck`
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui typecheck`
- `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend build:client`
- `bunx @biomejs/biome check ...` on the touched Cloud/shared/UI/app files
- `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend audit:cloud` produced `116 passed`
- `ELIZA_IOS_DERIVED_DATA_PATH=/Users/home/Documents/eliza/.eliza-local/DerivedData/ios-cloud-sim LANG=en_US.UTF-8 bun run --cwd packages/app build:ios:cloud:sim` succeeded

## Live Verification Notes

Before this PR is deployed to production, live `www.elizacloud.ai` still serves the old bundle and reproduces the mobile login failure. A direct live probe confirms the backend contract:

- query-bearing callback: `400 redirect_uri is not allowed for this tenant`
- bare `/login` callback: `302` to Google OAuth

After this lands and Cloudflare Pages production deploy completes, retest the iOS simulator Cloud login/provisioning path.

topPRs.title: fix(cloud-api): make container billing idempotent per period (no double-debit on cron re-run)
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## What

Hardens the daily container-billing cron so a re-run (manual trigger, overlapping CF cron, retry) can never double-debit. Defense in depth across three layers, plus a billing reconciliation fix.

**Stacks on #8269** (the free-hosting fix). Until that merges, the diff here includes its single commit; afterward this shrinks to just the four new commits.

## Why

The cron had no idempotency: `convertToCredits` re-debited earnings on every call, `listBillableContainers` ignored the (written-but-never-read) `next_billing_at`, there was no app-level lock (CF cron provides none), and `credit_transactions` recorded `-dailyCost` while `credit_balance` only moved by `-fromCredits` — so the ledger overstated the credit debit by the earnings portion (which is already on the earnings ledger), breaking `credit_balance == sum(credit_transactions)`.

## How

- **Deterministic period.** `computeContainerBillingPeriod(now)` normalizes the run to its UTC day, so the period (and the key derived from it) is stable across same-day re-runs.
- **Earnings idempotency.** `convertToCredits` takes an optional `idempotencyKey` (`container:<id>:<utc-day>`); under the per-user advisory lock it returns the prior `credit_conversion` ledger entry instead of debiting again. Backed by a partial unique index on `redeemable_earnings_ledger((metadata->>'idempotency_key'))`.
- **Query gate.** `listBillableContainers(now)` skips containers whose `next_billing_at` is still in the future (already paid this period).
- **Row-lock guard.** `recordSuccessfulDailyBilling` locks the container row and no-ops if the period is already billed — closing the read→write race between listing and charging. Backed by a partial unique index on `container_billing_records(container_id, billing_period_start) WHERE status='success'`.
- **Reconciliation.** The `credit_transaction` now records `-fromCredits` (the actual credit-balance movement); `dailyCost` and the earnings/credits split stay in metadata.

## Migration (0139)

Adds both partial unique indexes and **dedupes any pre-existing duplicate `success` rows first** (keep earliest by `created_at,id`) so the index can be created on existing prod data — confirmed duplicates can exist today since nothing guarded against them. The ledger index is partial on a non-null key, so existing conversions (which carry no key) are unaffected.

## Verification

- `db:migrate` applies all 140 migrations from scratch incl. 0139 against the real cumulative schema — clean.
- New PGlite-backed suite (`container-billing-idempotency.test.ts`) exercises the real SQL (`FOR UPDATE`, the JSONB-keyed lookup, both partial unique indexes): earnings debit once per key, gate by `next_billing_at`, `-fromCredits` recorded, row-lock no-op on re-run.
- `cloud-shared` + `cloud-api` typecheck clean; biome clean; `db:check-migrations` passes.

completedItems.title: fix(cloud): bump @stwd/sdk to 0.10.1 (passkey challengeId login fix)
completedItems.body: Prod passkey login fails with 'challengeId is required'. cloud-frontend bundled @stwd/sdk@0.8.1 whose published dist doesn't forward the passkey challengeId to /auth/passkey/login/verify. 0.10.1 (just published) adds it. Bumped cloud-fronte
